prompt:
Create a Y2K futurist Windows desktop remix of Van Gogh’s The Starry Night as a glowing cyberpunk operating-system screenshot. The canvas is a 4:3 desktop wallpaper in deep ultramarine, neon violet, electric blue, cyan, and gold, with painterly impasto brush strokes forming huge swirling star vortices across the sky. Preserve the Starry Night composition: a tall black cypress-like spire in the lower left foreground, rolling dark hills, a glowing village transformed into a futuristic city, and a river or bay reflecting purple-blue neon lights. Add transparent glassmorphism Windows-2000/XP style UI overlays with violet chrome, bloom, scanline shimmer, pixel fonts, and small title-bar buttons.

Desktop layout: a vertical translucent sidebar on the far left with exactly 8 desktop icons, from top to bottom labeled: “my pc”, “internet”, “e-mail”, “msn”, “music”, “pictures”, “shop”, “explore”. At the very top left place a window title bar labeled “Starry_Night.vin” with menu items “file”, “edit”, “view”, “tools”, “help”. Along the bottom place a glowing purple taskbar with a rounded “start” button on the left and a system tray on the right showing “02:37 AM”; include a recycle bin icon and label “recycle bin” near the bottom right.

Add exactly 3 floating windows on the right side. Window 1, top right: a music player titled “now playing”, with a small square album thumbnail, track text “vincent’s dream” and “sleepy gogh”, five empty star-rating outlines, timestamp “01:37 / 04:28”, a progress bar, and previous / pause / next controls. Window 2, mid-right: an info panel titled “about: painting.exe”, headed “THE STARRY NIGHT”, listing “vincent van gogh”, “1889”, “oil on canvas”, “remixed in: 3000”, “resolution: 1024x1280”, “color mode: neon rgb”, “size: 1.37 mb”, and bottom text “status: timeless.”; include a glowing wireframe globe graphic on the right side of this panel and a small heart outline in the lower right. Window 3, lower right: a notepad window titled “untitled - notepad” with menu items “file”, “edit”, “format”, “view”, “help”, containing the text “maybe the stars / are just windows / to other worlds. / / -vvg”.

Style constraints: make the whole image look like a nostalgic early-2000s vaporwave desktop interface merged with expressive oil paint; use luminous edges, transparent purple panels, crisp readable white pixel text, soft CRT glow, and no modern flat design. Keep all visible text exactly as specified and avoid adding extra windows, characters, logos, or watermarks.
